'''Hemei IV''' was a wealthy [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was the origin point of the [[Cosmic Balance]] religion. The [[Bakur Mining Corporation]] also originated there, and its Cosmic Balance–practicing [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officers]] believed that the world's affluence was disruptive. To remedy that state, the Corporation eventually led the [[Colonist/Legends|settlement]] of the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] planet [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. By more than a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] later, the Cosmic Balance had ceased to be practiced on Hemei IV.

==Description==
Hemei IV, also referred to simply as Hemei, was a terrestrial<ref name="GatORW" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="CSWE" /> in the [[Hemei system/Legends|Hemei system]], a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> Its ecology mirrored that of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a lush world of [[Forest/Legends|forests]], plains, [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:Bakura.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Hemei IV's ecology was similar to that of its colony planet Bakura (pictured).]]
The exploratory [[Mining/Legends|mining]] consortium [[Bakur Mining Corporation]]<ref name="GatORW" /> was at one point formed on Hemei IV.<ref name="CSWE" /> The top<ref name="GatORW" /> [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officers]]<ref name="CSWE" /> of the corporation,<ref name="GatORW" /> who were adherents of the local [[Cosmic Balance]] religion,<ref name="CSWE" /> believed that Hemei IV's concentration of wealth was disruptive to their faith's concept of "Balance." Consequently, in an attempt to remedy the situation, Bakur Mining Corporation [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] searched along the edge of the known [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] for a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] that could host a self-sufficient [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] that could in time become a mirror image of Hemei IV.<ref name="GatORW" />

Eventually, Bakura was determined to be a fitting candidate world,<ref name="GatORW" /> and in [[149 BBY/Legends|149 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', which is set during the events of ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'' and therefore in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] per ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', dates the [[Colonist/Legends|settlement]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] to 153 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to its setting.</ref> it was settled by a Bakur Mining Corporation [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ship]]<ref name="Truce">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref> of [[Human/Legends|Humans]]<ref name="GatORW" /> led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Deredith Arden]].<ref name="Truce" /> Bakura's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Salis D'aar/Legends|Salis D'aar]] eventually featured the site known as the [[Old Hemei Gardens]],<ref name="GatORW" /> with [[Bakuran]] [[Historian/Legends|historians]] tracing the colony's origins back to Hemei IV.<ref name="CSWE" /> By the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]<ref name="GatORW" /> of [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]–[[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the Cosmic Balance had become extinct on Hemei IV.<ref name="GatORW" />

==Inhabitants==
Hemei IV was an affluent world inhabited by Humans that served as the base of operations for the Bakur Mining Corporation.<ref name="GatORW" /> The Cosmic Balance religion emerged on the planet;<ref name="CSWE" /> however, the faith eventually ceased to be practiced there.<ref name="GatORW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hemei IV was first mentioned in [[Kathy Tyers]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]]'s [[1996]] ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'' of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Truce" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square M-12.<ref name="TEA" />
